_comment: Recommended npm packages to replicate Otter.ai's design in React
_source: Extracted from otter.ai tech stack analysis, 2026-03-28

styling._why: Otter uses custom Webflow CSS. Tailwind with their exact tokens is the fastest way to replicate in React.

animations._why: Otter uses GSAP for marquees, typewriter effects, and scroll-triggered animations. This is NOT optional — it's their core animation engine.

alternatives._note: Framer Motion is more React-idiomatic. Use if you prefer React hooks over GSAP timeline API. Both can achieve Otter's effects.

carousel._why: Otter uses Swiper.js for all carousels — testimonials, feature showcases, image galleries. Direct match.

option_a_gsap._note: Included with GSAP. Otter uses ScrollTrigger for scroll-driven marquee direction and section reveals.

option_b_standalone._note: Lighter alternative for simple fade-in-on-scroll. Otter also uses native IntersectionObserver for video autoplay.

icons._why: Otter uses custom SVG icons. Lucide is the closest open-source match in style — clean, minimal, consistent stroke width.

fonts._why: Otter's font is a custom Webflow font closest to Inter. Self-hosting with @fontsource is faster than Google Fonts.

utilities._why: For conditional class composition — essential when building Otter's component variants (btn-primary, btn-link, etc.)

optional_enhancements._note: If you want to add animated illustrations like many SaaS sites. Otter doesn't use Lottie but it fits the aesthetic.
optional_enhancements._note_radix: Headless, accessible components for FAQ accordions, nav dropdowns, pricing toggles. Otter builds these custom but Radix gives you accessibility for free.

install_command: npm install react react-dom react-router-dom gsap @gsap/react swiper lucide-react @fontsource/inter clsx tailwind-merge @radix-ui/react-accordion @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu
